title:
#1 NEW YORK TIMES BESTSELLER
HOW COUNTRIES
GO BROKE
The Big Cycle
gist: Promo/landing page for Ray Dalio's book 'How Countries Go Broke: The Big Cycle', dominated by endorsements and interview links. The genuine signal: Dalio argues debt crises follow a repeating 'Big Cycle' most people miss in real time, and he explicitly worries the US is on an unsustainable fiscal trajectory toward its own debt crisis, prescribing a '3% solution' to cut budget deficits.

insights.claim: Ray Dalio argues most countries experience debt crises and go broke for the same recurring reasons, but because the mechanics play out so infrequently most people fail to recognize them in real time.
insights.speaker: Ray Dalio
insights.so_what: If sovereign debt crises are mechanistic and repeating, they are forecastable; investors can position for the cycle stage rather than treat each crisis as a surprise.
insights.quote: Most countries experience debt crises and go broke for the same reasons. But because the mechanics at play occur so infrequently, most people don't recognize what's happening in real-time.

insights.claim: Dalio's track record cited as evidence: he anticipated the 2008 global financial crisis and the 2011 European debt crisis, lending weight to his framework's predictive claims.
insights.speaker: Ray Dalio
insights.so_what: Establishes the credibility behind the Big Cycle calls; useful context when weighing his current US warning.
insights.quote: Ray Dalio, legendary investor who anticipated the 2008 global financial crisis and 2011 European debt crisis

insights.claim: Dalio warns the US may be the next country to go broke, framing the national debt as a 'ticking debt bomb' and the biggest, most certain threat to US prosperity.
insights.speaker: Ray Dalio
insights.so_what: A high-conviction sovereign-debt warning from a cycle forecaster argues for hedging US Treasury/dollar exposure and watching deficit dynamics.
insights.quote: he worries the US may be next

insights.claim: Dalio prescribes a '3% solution' — reducing US budget deficits to roughly 3% of GDP — as the path for the US to avoid a debt crisis.
insights.speaker: Ray Dalio
insights.so_what: Gives a concrete fiscal benchmark to monitor; failure to approach a 3% deficit is, in Dalio's frame, the trigger condition for crisis.
insights.quote: the need for reducing budget deficits through his 3% solution

insights.claim: Dalio frames the choice as binary and urgent: the US must cut debt now or face an 'economic heart attack', tying fiscal policy to a breakdown of the existing monetary and geopolitical order.
insights.speaker: Ray Dalio
insights.so_what: Links US fiscal failure to monetary and geopolitical regime breakdown — a tail risk spanning rates, FX, and global order.
insights.quote: how the US must cut debt now or face an economic heart attack
insights.locator: World Governments Summit with Tucker Carlson
